seoTitle: Set Up Credit Cards with Sub-Accounts in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Learn how to set up credit card sub-accounts in QuickBooks Online for clearer expense tracking across departments or projects.
intro: Tracking credit card spend by department or project gets much easier with sub-accounts. This tutorial shows how to set up credit cards with sub-accounts in QuickBooks Online so your expense tracking and budgeting stay organized.

steps.heading: Navigate to QuickBooks settings
steps.body: Go to the account settings in QuickBooks Online where chart of accounts and credit cards are managed.

steps.heading: Create sub-accounts for credit cards
steps.body: Add new sub-accounts under your main credit card account to separate spending by department or purpose.

steps.heading: Manage transactions with sub-accounts
steps.body: Assign transactions to the appropriate sub-account as they come in to keep expense tracking accurate.

steps.heading: Use sub-accounts for organization
steps.body: Apply this structure for scenarios like tracking spending across multiple departments or projects within one business.

faq.question: Can I have multiple sub-accounts under one credit card account?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create as many sub-accounts as needed to break down spending categories.

faq.question: Do sub-account transactions roll up into the main account total?
faq.answer: Yes, sub-account balances combine into the parent account's total for overall reporting.

faq.question: Will setting up sub-accounts change my existing transaction history?
faq.answer: No, existing transactions stay as they are; sub-accounts affect how new transactions are categorized going forward.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for a single-person business?
faq.answer: It's most useful for businesses with multiple departments or projects, though even solo businesses can use it to separate personal and business spending categories.
